title: Create a refrigeration metering and monitoring baseline
mechanism: Measure electricity, temperature, defrost events, and operating patterns so waste can be identified before interventions are claimed.
preconditions: access to utility interval data or temporary metering
risk_notes: advisory only; no control change implied

title: Audit door seals, strip curtains, airlocks, and door discipline
mechanism: Reduce warm moist air infiltration so refrigeration load and frost formation decline.
causal_chain: less compressor runtime and frost burden
risk_notes: must not compromise emergency egress or food handling workflow

title: Review defrost schedules for over-defrosting
mechanism: Excess defrost adds heat and electrical load; review can identify unnecessary frequency or duration.
risk_notes: controls change requires expert review; food-safety constraints apply
evidence_gaps: coil frost data and product temperature logs

title: Clean condenser and evaporator coils and verify airflow paths
mechanism: Dirty coils and blocked airflow reduce heat-transfer efficiency and increase compressor/fan runtime.
risk_notes: maintenance should follow manufacturer and facility procedures

title: Switch off or schedule lighting and anti-condensation heaters where safe
mechanism: Lights and heaters add direct electricity load and heat that refrigeration must remove.
preconditions: confirm safety, visibility, and condensation requirements
risk_notes: must preserve safety and product integrity

title: Review thermostat, sensor calibration, and zone temperature policies
mechanism: Sensor drift or overcooling can cause unnecessary refrigeration while still appearing compliant.
risk_notes: no food-safety-critical setpoint changes without expert review

title: Evaluate floating head-pressure / condensing-pressure control audit
mechanism: Lower condensing pressure when ambient/load allow can reduce compressor lift and energy use.
risk_notes: CAUTION: controls change requires professional review

title: Assess evaporator fan scheduling or efficient fan motor opportunity
mechanism: Fans consume electricity and add heat; efficient motors or appropriate scheduling can reduce both loads.
causal_chain: less motor heat in cold space
risk_notes: must maintain air distribution and food-safety requirements

title: Reduce overstocking and blocked air-grille conditions
mechanism: Blocked grilles and overstocking impair airflow, increasing cooling time and temperature variability.

title: Create heat-recovery screening note for later engineering study
mechanism: Recovered compressor/condenser heat may offset hot-water, space-heating, or underfloor-heating loads.
risk_notes: screening only; not a retrofit recommendation
